planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Assess the stability and shortcut potential of the dominant destination port distribution in DDoS traffic.
key_checks: Measure class-conditioned distribution stability of Destination Port across time segments
Evaluate entropy and dominant port ratio variability within DDoS class
Test shortcut-like predictive leverage of Destination Port feature alone
Compare port distribution patterns between attack campaigns and benign traffic
success_criteria: Evidence of stable dominance of a single port in DDoS class across samples
Identification of potential shortcut exploitation via port-based heuristics
Detection of representation-sensitive shifts in port distribution under different conditions

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Characterize the nature and stability of class-conditioned structural variation in backward packet length features.
key_checks: Quantify variance ratios and unique value counts per class for backward packet length features
Assess dependency concentration and distribution shifts across network contexts
Investigate potential sampling or labeling biases influencing feature distributions
Examine local label-topology consistency around backward packet length features
success_criteria: Clear differentiation of genuine behavioral differences from dataset artifacts
Identification of unstable dependencies or representation shifts affecting these features
Evidence supporting or refuting sensitivity to sampling or labeling biases
router_constraints: preserve feature_scope for backward packet length features
preserve evidence refs e9 e10 e11 e12

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Evaluate the stability and shortcut risk of strong linear dependencies among packet length and related features.
key_checks: Measure correlation stability of feature pairs across classes and attack conditions
Analyze shortcut-like predictive leverage arising from these dependencies
Assess redundancy impact on effective dataset dimensionality
Compare dependency patterns between benign and DDoS classes
success_criteria: Confirmation of stable or shifting linear dependencies under different conditions
Identification of shortcut signals linked to these dependencies
Understanding of redundancy effects on model reliance and dataset structure
router_constraints: preserve feature_scope for correlated feature pairs
preserve evidence refs e13 e14 e15 e16 e17 e18 e19 e20 e21 e22
